title: PostgreSQL Default Credentials (postgres:postgres)
description: PostgreSQL 8.3.1 is accessible remotely with the default postgres:postgres credentials. The postgres superuser account has full administrative privileges over all databases. While COPY FROM PROGRAM is not available in this version, full database access enables data theft and modification.
evidence:
Exploitation command:
$ psql "host=192.168.0.140 user=postgres password=postgres sslmode=disable" -c "SELECT version();" -c "SELECT usename FROM pg_user;"

Output:
version
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PostgreSQL 8.3.1 on i486-pc-linux-gnu, compiled by GCC cc (GCC) 4.2.3 (Ubuntu 4.2.3-2ubuntu4)
(1 row)

usename
----------
postgres
(1 row)

Full superuser access confirmed with default credentials.
remediation: Change the default PostgreSQL password immediately. Restrict remote access via pg_hba.conf to trusted hosts only. Disable remote connections if not needed. Use strong authentication methods.

issues: HIGH severity finding should have stronger evidence
Exploitation proof required for high/critical findings
suggestions: Add command output demonstrating access or data extraction
For high findings, demonstrate actual impact
Actually exploit the vulnerability and capture output
For SQLi: Show extracted data or UNION-based output
